outcome_test.go

1	// Package try defines the sealed Outcome sum type that the execute-bead
2	// orchestrator will return once the C-series refactor lands. The types in
3	// this file are introduced ahead of any caller (see ddx-240f2082) so that
4	// later beads can migrate the orchestrator to produce Outcome values
5	// without simultaneously inventing the vocabulary.
6	package try
7	
8	import (
9		"fmt"
10		"time"
11	
12		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
13		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
14	)
15	
16	// Disposition is the closed set of post-attempt routing decisions the
17	// execute-loop can take on a bead. Every Outcome carries exactly one.
18	type Disposition int
19	
20	const (
21		DispositionMerged Disposition = iota
22		DispositionAlreadyDone
23		DispositionRetry
24		DispositionPark
25		DispositionNeedsHuman
26		DispositionLoopError
27	)
28	
29	func (d Disposition) String() string {
30		switch d {
31		case DispositionMerged:
32			return "merged"
33		case DispositionAlreadyDone:
34			return "already_done"
35		case DispositionRetry:
36			return "retry"
37		case DispositionPark:
38			return "park"
39		case DispositionNeedsHuman:
40			return "needs_human"
41		case DispositionLoopError:
42			return "loop_error"
43		default:
44			return fmt.Sprintf("disposition(%d)", int(d))
45		}
46	}
47	
48	// ParkReason enumerates the closed vocabulary of reasons the loop may park
49	// a bead. String-typed for ergonomic comparison, but every value used in
50	// production must come from the constants below; ParkReasonValid enforces
51	// that at the boundary.
52	type ParkReason string
53	
54	const (
55		ParkReasonNeedsReview              ParkReason = "needs_review"
56		ParkReasonDecomposition            ParkReason = "decomposition"
57		ParkReasonPushFailed               ParkReason = "push_failed"
58		ParkReasonPushConflict             ParkReason = "push_conflict"
59		ParkReasonCostCap                  ParkReason = "cost_cap"
60		ParkReasonLoopError                ParkReason = "loop_error"
61		ParkReasonNoChangesUnverified      ParkReason = "no_changes_unverified"
62		ParkReasonNoChangesUnjustified     ParkReason = "no_changes_unjustified"
63		ParkReasonRateLimitBudgetExhausted ParkReason = "rate_limit_budget_exhausted"
64		ParkReasonQuotaPaused              ParkReason = "quota_paused"
65		ParkReasonLockContention           ParkReason = "lock_contention"
66	)
67	
68	// AllParkReasons returns the canonical list of ParkReason values in a
69	// stable order. Used by tests and by callers that need to enumerate the
70	// vocabulary (e.g. for documentation generators).
71	func AllParkReasons() []ParkReason {
72		return []ParkReason{
73			ParkReasonNeedsReview,
74			ParkReasonDecomposition,
75			ParkReasonPushFailed,
76			ParkReasonPushConflict,
77			ParkReasonCostCap,
78			ParkReasonLoopError,
79			ParkReasonNoChangesUnverified,
80			ParkReasonNoChangesUnjustified,
81			ParkReasonRateLimitBudgetExhausted,
82			ParkReasonQuotaPaused,
83			ParkReasonLockContention,
84		}
85	}
86	
87	// ParkReasonValid reports whether r is a member of the closed ParkReason
88	// vocabulary. The empty string is invalid; use a zero ParkReason only on
89	// non-Park dispositions.
90	func ParkReasonValid(r ParkReason) bool {
91		for _, p := range AllParkReasons() {
92			if p == r {
93				return true
94			}
95		}
96		return false
97	}
98	
99	func (p ParkReason) String() string { return string(p) }
100	
101	// Outcome is the sealed sum type the execute-loop orchestrator will return
102	// for each bead attempt. It carries the routing decision (Disposition)
103	// along with the small set of facts the loop needs to apply that decision:
104	// timing, cost, the resulting commit (if any), and the bead-tracker events
105	// to record.
106	type Outcome struct {
107		BeadID       string
108		AttemptID    string
109		BaseRev      string
110		ResultRev    string
111		SessionID    string
112		DurationMS   int64
113		CostUSD      float64
114		Disposition  Disposition
115		Cooldown     time.Duration
116		ParkReason   ParkReason
117		RecordEvents []bead.BeadEvent
118	}
119	
120	// dispositionToStatus maps a (Disposition, ParkReason) pair onto the
121	// canonical execute-bead status string. The mapping is deterministic and
122	// invertible for the canonical reports produced by FromOutcome.
123	func dispositionToStatus(d Disposition, p ParkReason) string {
124		switch d {
125		case DispositionMerged:
126			return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
127		case DispositionAlreadyDone:
128			return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied
129		case DispositionRetry:
130			return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
131		case DispositionNeedsHuman:
132			return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictNeedsHuman
133		case DispositionLoopError:
134			return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
135		case DispositionPark:
136			switch p {
137			case ParkReasonNeedsReview:
138				return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
139			case ParkReasonDecomposition:
140				return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition
141			case ParkReasonPushFailed:
142				return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed
143			case ParkReasonPushConflict:
144				return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict
145			case ParkReasonNoChangesUnverified, ParkReasonNoChangesUnjustified:
146				return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
147			default:
148				// Park reasons without a dedicated status share execution_failed
149				// and rely on Detail (= ParkReason) to disambiguate on inverse.
150				return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
151			}
152		}
153		return ""
154	}
155	
156	// FromOutcome produces the canonical ExecuteBeadReport that ToOutcome
157	// inverts. RecordEvents are not carried (ExecuteBeadReport has no events
158	// field); callers that need events handle them out-of-band.
159	func FromOutcome(o Outcome) agent.ExecuteBeadReport {
160		rep := agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
161			BeadID:     o.BeadID,
162			AttemptID:  o.AttemptID,
163			Status:     dispositionToStatus(o.Disposition, o.ParkReason),
164			SessionID:  o.SessionID,
165			BaseRev:    o.BaseRev,
166			ResultRev:  o.ResultRev,
167			CostUSD:    o.CostUSD,
168			DurationMS: o.DurationMS,
169		}
170		if o.Cooldown > 0 {
171			rep.RetryAfter = o.Cooldown.String()
172		}
173		if o.Disposition == DispositionPark && o.ParkReason != "" {
174			rep.Detail = string(o.ParkReason)
175		}
176		return rep
177	}
178	
179	// ToOutcome decodes an ExecuteBeadReport back into an Outcome. It is the
180	// inverse of FromOutcome on canonical reports; reports produced by other
181	// code paths may carry fields Outcome does not model, which are dropped.
182	func ToOutcome(r agent.ExecuteBeadReport) Outcome {
183		o := Outcome{
184			BeadID:     r.BeadID,
185			AttemptID:  r.AttemptID,
186			BaseRev:    r.BaseRev,
187			ResultRev:  r.ResultRev,
188			SessionID:  r.SessionID,
189			DurationMS: r.DurationMS,
190			CostUSD:    r.CostUSD,
191		}
192		if r.RetryAfter != "" {
193			if d, err := time.ParseDuration(r.RetryAfter); err == nil {
194				o.Cooldown = d
195			}
196		}
197		switch r.Status {
198		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess:
199			o.Disposition = DispositionMerged
200		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
201			o.Disposition = DispositionAlreadyDone
202		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges:
203			// Detail carrying a valid ParkReason promotes to Park; otherwise Retry.
204			if pr := ParkReason(r.Detail); ParkReasonValid(pr) &&
205				(pr == ParkReasonNoChangesUnverified || pr == ParkReasonNoChangesUnjustified) {
206				o.Disposition = DispositionPark
207				o.ParkReason = pr
208			} else {
209				o.Disposition = DispositionRetry
210			}
211		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview:
212			o.Disposition = DispositionPark
213			o.ParkReason = ParkReasonNeedsReview
214		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition:
215			o.Disposition = DispositionPark
216			o.ParkReason = ParkReasonDecomposition
217		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed:
218			o.Disposition = DispositionPark
219			o.ParkReason = ParkReasonPushFailed
220		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict:
221			o.Disposition = DispositionPark
222			o.ParkReason = ParkReasonPushConflict
223		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictNeedsHuman:
224			o.Disposition = DispositionNeedsHuman
225		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed:
226			// Detail carrying a valid ParkReason disambiguates Park from LoopError.
227			if pr := ParkReason(r.Detail); ParkReasonValid(pr) {
228				o.Disposition = DispositionPark
229				o.ParkReason = pr
230			} else {
231				o.Disposition = DispositionLoopError
232			}
233		}
234		return o
235	}
236

cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:620:func ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, beadID string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadRuntime, gitOps GitOps) (*ExecuteBeadResult, error) {
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:176:func LandBeadResult(projectRoot string, res *ExecuteBeadResult, gitOps OrchestratorGitOps, opts BeadLandingOptions) (*BeadLandingResult, error) {

580		if err != nil {
581			return
582		}
583		var summary string
584		if body.CostUSD > 0 {
585			summary = fmt.Sprintf("tokens=%d cost_usd=%.4f", body.TotalTokens, body.CostUSD)
586		} else {
587			summary = fmt.Sprintf("tokens=%d", body.TotalTokens)
588		}
589		if body.Model != "" {
590			summary += fmt.Sprintf(" model=%s", body.Model)
591		}
592		_ = appender.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
593			Kind:    "cost",
594			Summary: summary,
595			Body:    string(data),
596			Actor:   "ddx",
597			Source:  "ddx agent execute-bead",
598		})
599	}
600	
601	// ExecuteBeadWithConfig is the thin worker: it creates an isolated worktree,
602	// constructs the agent prompt from bead context, runs the agent harness,
603	// synthesizes a commit if the agent left uncommitted changes, then cleans up
604	// the worktree and returns the result. It classifies outcomes as exactly one
605	// of:
606	//
607	//   - task_succeeded: agent exited 0 and produced one or more commits
608	//   - task_failed:    agent exited non-zero
609	//   - task_no_changes: agent exited 0, made no commits, and wrote a rationale
610	//   - task_no_evidence: agent exited 0 but made no commits and wrote no rationale
611	//
612	// Merge, UpdateRef, gate evaluation, preserve-ref management, and orphan
613	// recovery are the parent's responsibility (see LandBeadResult, RecoverOrphans).
614	//
615	// Agent dispatch: production callers leave runtime.Service and runtime.AgentRunner
616	// nil. ExecuteBeadWithConfig constructs a fresh agentlib.FizeauService from
617	// projectRoot via NewServiceFromWorkDir and dispatches via RunViaServiceWith.
618	// Tests may set runtime.AgentRunner to inject a fake that returns canned
619	// Result values; when set, it takes precedence over the service path.
620	func ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, beadID string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadRuntime, gitOps GitOps) (*ExecuteBeadResult, error) {
621		if ctx == nil {
622			ctx = context.Background()
623		}
624		attemptID := GenerateAttemptID()
625		if runtime.WorkerID == "" {
626			runtime.WorkerID = os.Getenv("DDX_WORKER_ID")
627		}
628	
629		wtPath := executeBeadWorktreePath(beadID, attemptID)
630		if mkErr := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(wtPath), 0o755); mkErr != nil {
631			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating execute-bead worktree parent dir: %w", mkErr)
632		}
633	
634		// Commit beads.jsonl before spawning worktree so the worktree snapshot
635		// includes any bead metadata updates (e.g. spec-id).
636		if err := CommitTracker(projectRoot); err != nil {
637			return nil, err
638		}
639	
640		// Checkpoint any remaining caller dirt as a real commit on the current
641		// branch (FEAT-012 §22, US-126 AC#1). The new HEAD becomes the effective
642		// base for the worker worktree; caller's edits are preserved as a normal
643		// commit they can `git reset HEAD~` if they want them back uncommitted.
644		if _, err := gitOps.SynthesizeCommit(projectRoot, "chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead "+attemptID); err != nil {
645			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pre-execute-bead checkpoint: %w", err)
646		}
647	
648		// Resolve base revision after the tracker + checkpoint commits.
649		baseRev, err := resolveBase(gitOps, projectRoot, runtime.FromRev)
650		if err != nil {
651			return nil, err
652		}
653	
654		// Create the isolated worktree. Orphan recovery is the parent's responsibility
655		// (call RecoverOrphans before invoking workers).
656		if err := gitOps.WorktreeAdd(projectRoot, wtPath, baseRev); err != nil {
657			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating isolated worktree: %w", err)
658		}
659		defer func() {
660			_ = gitOps.WorktreeRemove(projectRoot, wtPath)
661		}()
662	
663		// Publish the live run-state so operators and HELIX can observe what is
664		// executing without polling the bead tracker (CONTRACT-001 §5). The file
665		// is removed on completion; a crashed worker leaves a stale file that
666		// RecoverOrphans sweeps before the next attempt.
667		_ = WriteRunState(projectRoot, RunState{
668			BeadID:       beadID,
669			AttemptID:    attemptID,
670			Harness:      rcfg.Harness(),
671			Model:        rcfg.Model(),
672			StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
673			WorktreePath: wtPath,
674		})
675		defer func() {
676			_ = ClearRunState(projectRoot)
677		}()
678	
679		// Repair project-local skill symlinks whose targets do not resolve inside
680		// the freshly created worktree.
681		_ = materializeWorktreeSkills(wtPath)
682	
683		// Prepare artifacts (context load, prompt generation).
684		artifacts, err := prepareArtifacts(projectRoot, wtPath, beadID, attemptID, baseRev, rcfg, runtime)
685		if err != nil {
686			res := &ExecuteBeadResult{
687				BeadID:    beadID,
688				AttemptID: attemptID,
689				WorkerID:  runtime.WorkerID,
690				BaseRev:   baseRev,
691				ResultRev: baseRev, // no commits; ResultRev == BaseRev signals no output
692				ExitCode:  1,
693				Error:     err.Error(),
694				Outcome:   ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed,
695			}
696			if abInfo, _ := os.Stat(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID)); abInfo != nil && abInfo.IsDir() {
697				res.ExecutionDir = filepath.Join(ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID)
698			}
699			res.FailureMode = ClassifyFailureMode(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Error)

100	
101	// RealOrchestratorGitOps implements OrchestratorGitOps via os/exec git commands.
102	type RealOrchestratorGitOps struct{}
103	
104	// UpdateRef updates a git ref to point at sha.
105	func (r *RealOrchestratorGitOps) UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error {
106		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "update-ref", ref, sha).CombinedOutput()
107		if err != nil {
108			return fmt.Errorf("git update-ref %s: %s: %w", ref, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
109		}
110		return nil
111	}
112	
113	// BeadLandingOptions controls how the orchestrator lands a completed worker result.
114	type BeadLandingOptions struct {
115		// NoMerge skips the land step and preserves the result under
116		// refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/... instead.
117		NoMerge bool
118	
119		// WtPath is the path to a worktree checked out at ResultRev, used for gate
120		// evaluation. When empty, gate evaluation is skipped (the worktree has
121		// typically been cleaned up by the time the orchestrator runs).
122		WtPath string
123	
124		// GovernIDs are the governing artifact IDs to use for gate evaluation.
125		// Only used when WtPath is non-empty. Typically extracted from the worker's
126		// manifest artifact via ExtractGoverningIDsFromManifest.
127		GovernIDs []string
128	
129		// ChecksArtifactPath is the absolute path to write checks.json. Optional.
130		ChecksArtifactPath string
131		// ChecksArtifactRel is the relative path stored in the result for checks.json.
132		ChecksArtifactRel string
133	
134		// LandingAdvancer, when non-nil, replaces the old in-process Merge step
135		// with the coordinator-pattern Land() call. The callback is expected to
136		// run fetch → (ff or merge) → push serialized against other submissions
137		// for the same projectRoot. When nil, LandBeadResult falls back to
138		// preserving the result under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/...
139		// rather than touching the target branch.
140		LandingAdvancer func(res *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error)
141	}
142	
143	// BeadLandingResult records the outcome of the orchestrator's landing step.
144	type BeadLandingResult struct {
145		// Outcome is one of: "merged", "preserved", "no-changes".
146		Outcome string `json:"outcome"`
147		// Reason is a human-readable explanation of the outcome.
148		Reason string `json:"reason,omitempty"`
149		// PreserveRef is set when the result was preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/...
150		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
151		// GateResults holds the outcome of each required execution gate.
152		GateResults []GateCheckResult `json:"gate_results,omitempty"`
153		// RequiredExecSummary is "pass", "fail", or "skipped".
154		RequiredExecSummary string `json:"required_exec_summary,omitempty"`
155		// ChecksFile is the relative path to checks.json when gate results were written.
156		ChecksFile string `json:"checks_file,omitempty"`
157		// RatchetEvidence aggregates every ratchet-evaluated gate's evidence for
158		// the attempt. Surfaced separately from GateResults so HELIX and other
159		// consumers can tell apart ratchet outcomes from generic gate failures.
160		RatchetEvidence []RatchetEvidence `json:"ratchet_evidence,omitempty"`
161		// RatchetSummary is "pass", "fail", or "" (no ratchets evaluated).
162		RatchetSummary string `json:"ratchet_summary,omitempty"`
163	}
164	
165	// LandBeadResult is the parent-side orchestrator. It receives a completed worker
166	// result and decides whether to merge, preserve, or report no-changes. All
167	// Merge, UpdateRef, gate evaluation, and preserve-ref management live here.
168	// The worker (ExecuteBead) must not call any of these operations.
169	//
170	// Outcome rules:
171	//   - ResultRev == BaseRev → "no-changes" (agent made no commits)
172	//   - ExitCode != 0 with commits → "preserved" (agent failed but left output)
173	//   - Gate failed → "preserved"
174	//   - NoMerge → "preserved"
175	//   - Default → attempt merge; on conflict → "preserved"
176	func LandBeadResult(projectRoot string, res *ExecuteBeadResult, gitOps OrchestratorGitOps, opts BeadLandingOptions) (*BeadLandingResult, error) {
177		landing := &BeadLandingResult{}
178	
179		// Agent failed with no commits: report as error (not no-changes).
180		// Prefer res.Reason (e.g. HeadRev failure set by worker) over res.Error
181		// (agent error) so the primary context failure is surfaced as the reason.
182		if res.ExitCode != 0 && res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
183			landing.Outcome = "error"
184			switch {
185			case res.Reason != "":
186				landing.Reason = res.Reason
187			case res.Error != "":
188				landing.Reason = res.Error
189			default:
190				landing.Reason = "agent execution failed"
191			}
192			return landing, nil
193		}
194	
195		// No changes from the worker: nothing to land.
196		if res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
197			if res.Outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence || res.FailureMode == FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced {
198				landing.Outcome = "no-evidence"
199				if res.Reason != "" {
200					landing.Reason = res.Reason
201				} else if res.Error != "" {
202					landing.Reason = res.Error
203				} else {
204					landing.Reason = "agent exited without a commit or no_changes_rationale.txt"
205				}
206				return landing, nil
207			}
208			landing.Outcome = "no-changes"
209			if res.NoChangesRationale != "" {
210				landing.Reason = res.NoChangesRationale
211			} else {
212				landing.Reason = "agent made no commits"
213			}
214			return landing, nil
215		}
216	
217		// Agent failed but produced commits: preserve without attempting merge.
218		if res.ExitCode != 0 {
219			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
220			if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
221				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref: %w", err)
222			}
223			landing.Outcome = "preserved"
224			landing.PreserveRef = ref
225			landing.Reason = "agent execution failed"
226			return landing, nil
227		}
228	
229		// Evaluate required gates when a worktree path and governing IDs are
230		// provided. EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult is the canonical helper —
231		// shared with upstream callers that want gate eval before submitting
232		// through the coordinator. It mutates res.GateResults / RequiredExecSummary
233		// / RatchetEvidence / RatchetSummary / ChecksFile in place; we mirror the
234		// outputs onto landing so ApplyLandingToResult can copy them back later
235		// without losing the existing landing.* contract.
236		anyGateFailed, anyRatchetFailed, gateErr := EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult(
237			opts.WtPath, opts.GovernIDs, res, projectRoot,
238			opts.ChecksArtifactPath, opts.ChecksArtifactRel,
239		)
240		if gateErr != nil {
241			return nil, gateErr
242		}
243		gateResults := res.GateResults
244		landing.GateResults = gateResults
245		landing.RequiredExecSummary = res.RequiredExecSummary
246		landing.RatchetEvidence = res.RatchetEvidence
247		landing.RatchetSummary = res.RatchetSummary
248		landing.ChecksFile = res.ChecksFile
249	
250		// Gate failed: preserve instead of merging. Ratchet misses get a
251		// dedicated reason so status/failure_mode classifiers can distinguish
252		// them from generic command failures.
253		if anyGateFailed {
254			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
255			if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
256				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref: %w", err)
257			}
258			landing.Outcome = "preserved"
259			landing.PreserveRef = ref
260			if anyRatchetFailed {
261				landing.Reason = RatchetPreserveReason
262			} else {
263				landing.Reason = "post-run checks failed"
264			}
265			return landing, nil
266		}
267	
268		// --no-merge: preserve unconditionally.
269		if opts.NoMerge {
270			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
271			if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
272				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref: %w", err)
273			}
274			landing.Outcome = "preserved"
275			landing.PreserveRef = ref
276			landing.Reason = "--no-merge specified"
277			return landing, nil
278		}
279	
280		// Default: land the worker's commits on the target branch. When a
281		// LandingAdvancer is provided (server coordinator / --local coordinator)
282		// it runs the fetch → (ff or merge) → push sequence serialized per
283		// projectRoot. When no advancer is provided, LandBeadResult falls back
284		// to preserving the result under refs/ddx/iterations/ — the interactive
285		// single-bead CLI path, which intentionally does NOT auto-advance the
286		// target branch (the operator moves the ref themselves).
287		if opts.LandingAdvancer != nil {
288			land, landErr := opts.LandingAdvancer(res)
289			if landErr != nil {
290				return nil, fmt.Errorf("land advancer: %w", landErr)
291			}
292			switch land.Status {
293			case "landed":
294				landing.Outcome = "merged"
295				if land.Merged {
296					landing.Reason = "merged onto current tip"
297				}
298				if land.NewTip != "" {
299					res.ResultRev = land.NewTip

280		Heartbeat bool      `json:"heartbeat"`
281		TS        time.Time `json:"ts"`
282		ElapsedMS int64     `json:"elapsed_ms"`
283		Message   string    `json:"message,omitempty"`
284	}
285

287		Attempts          int                  `json:"attempts"`

297	// ExecuteBeadWorker drains the current single-project execution-ready queue.
298	// It intentionally does not retry a failed/conflicted bead again in the same
299	// process run; a later operator-driven invocation can create the next attempt.
300	type ExecuteBeadWorker struct {
301		Store               ExecuteBeadLoopStore
302		Executor            ExecuteBeadExecutor
303		SatisfactionChecker SatisfactionChecker // nil → NoChangesContract default
304		// VerificationRunner runs a verification_command parsed out of a
305		// no_changes_rationale.txt under NoChangesContract (TD-031 §8.1). When
306		// nil, DefaultVerificationCommandRunner is used.
307		VerificationRunner VerificationCommandRunner
308		Now                func() time.Time
309		// Reviewer, when non-nil, is called after every successful merge to
310		// validate the commit against the bead's acceptance criteria. When nil,
311		// post-merge review is skipped (same behaviour as --no-review).
312		Reviewer BeadReviewer
313	
314		// ConflictResolver, when non-nil, is called after the 3-way ort auto-merge
315		// step fails to recover a preserved iteration. The implementation should
316		// attempt a focused conflict-resolution pass (e.g. a cheap-tier agent run
317		// with the conflict files and bead AC) and return the new merged tip SHA on
318		// success. isBlocking=true signals the conflict requires human judgment
319		// (escalating to land_conflict_needs_human); false means failed-but-retriable.
320		ConflictResolver func(ctx context.Context, beadID, preserveRef, projectRoot string) (newTip string, isBlocking bool, err error)
321	
322		// ComplexityGate, when non-nil, is invoked between RoutePreflight and Claim.
323		// It evaluates whether the candidate bead is atomic (proceed to Claim),
324		// decomposable (file children, block parent, re-pick), or ambiguous
325		// (surface to human triage). When nil, the gate is bypassed and a one-time
326		// warning is emitted per Run() invocation. See triage.go and
327		// NewComplexityGate for the standard implementation.
328		ComplexityGate TriageGate
329	
330		// conflictAutoRecoverFn replaces the default landConflictAutoRecover. Set
331		// in tests to inject controlled recovery results without a real git repo.
332		conflictAutoRecoverFn func(wd, preserveRef string, gitOps LandingGitOps) (string, error)
333	}

349		return ProgressEvent{

370	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) Run(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
371		if w.Store == nil {
372			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: store is required")
373		}
374		if w.Executor == nil {
375			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: executor is required")
376		}
377	
378		now := w.Now
379		if now == nil {
380			now = time.Now
381		}
382		assignee := rcfg.Assignee()
383		if assignee == "" {
384			assignee = "ddx"
385		}
386		noProgressCooldown := rcfg.NoProgressCooldown()
387		if noProgressCooldown <= 0 {
388			noProgressCooldown = 6 * time.Hour
389		}
390		maxNoChangesBeforeClose := rcfg.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose()
391		if maxNoChangesBeforeClose <= 0 {
392			maxNoChangesBeforeClose = 3
393		}
394		heartbeatInterval := rcfg.HeartbeatInterval()
395		if heartbeatInterval <= 0 {
396			heartbeatInterval = bead.HeartbeatInterval
397		}
398		harness := rcfg.Harness()
399		model := rcfg.Model()

180		// model-gave-up: the model was prevented from making progress by an
181		// external cause (context cancellation, executor SIGKILL/SIGTERM,
182		// transport-class provider error, server restart, routing preflight
183		// rejection). The execute-loop bypasses the no-progress cooldown for
184		// Disrupted reports so the bead is immediately re-claimable instead of
185		// being parked for hours on a transient outage. ddx-5b3e57f4.
186		Disrupted bool `json:"disrupted,omitempty"`
187		// DisruptionReason carries the kind of disruption detected when
188		// Disrupted=true (e.g. "context_canceled", "context_deadline",
189		// "transport_error", "preflight_rejected"). Used in the
190		// `disruption_detected` event body so operators can see which class is
191		// occurring.
192		DisruptionReason string `json:"disruption_reason,omitempty"`
193	}

205	// SatisfactionChecker evaluates whether a bead that returned no_changes is
206	// already satisfied and should be closed, or is still unresolved and should
207	// receive retry suppression. noChangesCount is the cumulative count including

210	// When satisfied is true the caller closes the bead with the returned evidence
211	// string as the detail. When false the caller applies a retry cooldown and
212	// leaves the bead open.
213	type SatisfactionChecker interface {
214		CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (satisfied bool, evidence string, err error)
215	}

219	
220	func (f SatisfactionCheckerFunc) CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (bool, string, error) {
221		return f(ctx, beadID, noChangesCount)
222	}

cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:125:type ExecuteBeadReport struct {

125	type ExecuteBeadReport struct {
126		BeadID    string `json:"bead_id"`
127		AttemptID string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
128		WorkerID  string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
129		Harness   string `json:"harness,omitempty"`
130		Provider  string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
131		Model     string `json:"model,omitempty"`
132		// ActualPower is the routing-decision power of the model that actually
133		// served the implementer's call. Forwarded to the post-merge reviewer so
134		// it can request MinPower=actualPower+1 and bias routing toward a
135		// stronger reviewer (R4 pairing).
136		ActualPower int    `json:"actual_power,omitempty"`
137		Status      string `json:"status"`
138		Detail      string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
139		SessionID   string `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
140		BaseRev     string `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`
141		ResultRev   string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
142		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
143		RetryAfter  string `json:"retry_after,omitempty"`
144		// NoChangesRationale carries the agent's explanation when status == no_changes.
145		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`
146		// ReviewVerdict is the post-merge review verdict (APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES,
147		// or BLOCK) when a reviewer ran. Empty when review was skipped.
148		ReviewVerdict string `json:"review_verdict,omitempty"`
149		// ReviewRationale carries the actionable reviewer-authored findings for
150		// non-APPROVE review outcomes.
151		ReviewRationale string `json:"review_rationale,omitempty"`
152		// Tier is the model tier used for the final attempt (cheap, standard, smart).
153		// Populated by tier-escalating executors; empty for single-tier attempts.
154		Tier string `json:"tier,omitempty"`
155		// ProbeResult is a brief summary of the provider health probe at attempt time.
156		ProbeResult string `json:"probe_result,omitempty"`
157		// CostUSD is the dollar cost of this attempt as reported by the harness.
158		// Tier-escalating executors propagate this so the escalation trace can
159		// compute wasted/effective spend.
160		CostUSD float64 `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
161		// DurationMS is the wall-clock duration of this attempt.
162		DurationMS int64 `json:"duration_ms,omitempty"`
163		// Profile routing telemetry. Populated when execute-loop uses a profile
164		// ladder rather than an explicit harness/model pin.
165		RequestedProfile string `json:"requested_profile,omitempty"`
166		RequestedTier    string `json:"requested_tier,omitempty"`
167		ResolvedTier     string `json:"resolved_tier,omitempty"`
168		EscalationCount  int    `json:"escalation_count,omitempty"`
169		FinalTier        string `json:"final_tier,omitempty"`
170		// DecompositionRecommendation carries the structured list of recommended
171		// sub-bead titles when Status == declined_needs_decomposition. The loop
172		// records these on the bead as a `decomposition-recommendation` event so
173		// operators (or helix-evolve) can split the bead without re-deriving the
174		// recommendation.
175		DecompositionRecommendation []string `json:"decomposition_recommendation,omitempty"`
176		// DecompositionRationale is a free-form explanation accompanying
177		// DecompositionRecommendation. Optional.
178		DecompositionRationale string `json:"decomposition_rationale,omitempty"`
179		// Disrupted marks a failed attempt as worker-disrupted rather than
180		// model-gave-up: the model was prevented from making progress by an
181		// external cause (context cancellation, executor SIGKILL/SIGTERM,
182		// transport-class provider error, server restart, routing preflight
183		// rejection). The execute-loop bypasses the no-progress cooldown for
184		// Disrupted reports so the bead is immediately re-claimable instead of
185		// being parked for hours on a transient outage. ddx-5b3e57f4.
186		Disrupted bool `json:"disrupted,omitempty"`
187		// DisruptionReason carries the kind of disruption detected when
188		// Disrupted=true (e.g. "context_canceled", "context_deadline",
189		// "transport_error", "preflight_rejected"). Used in the
190		// `disruption_detected` event body so operators can see which class is
191		// occurring.
192		DisruptionReason string `json:"disruption_reason,omitempty"`
193	}

196		Execute(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error)
197	}

28	// ChecksFile) via ApplyLandingToResult before the result is written to disk or

30	type ExecuteBeadResult struct {

36	
37		// Outcome and Status are initially set by the worker to task-level values
38		// (task_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes), then overwritten by
39		// ApplyLandingToResult with the landing decision (merged / preserved /
40		// no-changes) so callers see a unified record.
41		Outcome string `json:"outcome"`
42		Status  string `json:"status,omitempty"`
43		Detail  string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
44

53		// consumers use these to distinguish ratchet-preserved attempts from

95	// BeadEventAppender records append-only evidence events on a bead.

97	// package does not need to import a concrete store type in tests.
98	type BeadEventAppender interface {

100	}
101

103	// Implemented by *bead.Store. ADR-022 §Cancel SLA: the worker polls
104	// IsCancelRequested mid-attempt and on a positive read writes

106	type BeadCancelStore interface {

108		MarkCancelHonored(id string) error

115	
116	// ExecuteBeadRuntime carries the non-durable plumbing for an execute-bead
117	// run: per-invocation intent (FromRev, PromptFile, WorkerID) and
118	// non-serializable injection seams (BeadEvents, Service, AgentRunner).
119	// Durable knobs (Harness, Model, Provider, ModelRef, Effort,
120	// ContextBudget, MirrorCfg) live on config.ResolvedConfig and are passed
121	// via ExecuteBeadWithConfig's rcfg argument.
122	//
123	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Stage 3.
124	type ExecuteBeadRuntime struct {
125		FromRev     string // base git revision (default: HEAD)
126		PromptFile  string // override prompt file (auto-generated if empty)
127		WorkerID    string // from DDX_WORKER_ID env or caller
128		BeadEvents  BeadEventAppender
129		BeadCancel  BeadCancelStore // optional: enables operator-cancel mid-attempt poll
130		Service     agentlib.FizeauService

132	}

134	// GitOps abstracts the git operations required by the worker.
135	// Merge is intentionally excluded — that belongs to the parent-side
136	// orchestrator (OrchestratorGitOps). UpdateRef/DeleteRef are exposed here
137	// so landing-side helpers (e.g. BuildLandingGateContext) can pin a
138	// transient ref while running gate evaluation against an ephemeral
139	// worktree.
140	type GitOps interface {
141		HeadRev(dir string) (string, error)
142		ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error)
143		WorktreeAdd(dir, wtPath, rev string) error
144		WorktreeRemove(dir, wtPath string) error
145		WorktreeList(dir string) ([]string, error)
146		WorktreePrune(dir string) error
147		IsDirty(dir string) (bool, error)

31	// ImplementerRouting captures the implementer's resolved routing details so
32	// the post-merge reviewer can build a paired ExecuteRequest with Role=reviewer,
33	// the same correlation, and MinPower bumped one above the implementer's
34	// actual selected power (R4 pairing).
35	type ImplementerRouting struct {
36		Harness     string
37		Provider    string
38		Model       string

41		// its keys into its own dispatch metadata so events / session log /
42		// aggregations can join the two calls.
43		Correlation map[string]string
44	}
45	
46	// Verdict is the outcome of a post-merge bead review.
47	type Verdict string
48	
49	const (
50		// VerdictApprove means all AC items passed; the bead stays closed.
51		VerdictApprove Verdict = "APPROVE"
52		// VerdictRequestChanges means some AC items need fixing; the bead is reopened.
53		VerdictRequestChanges Verdict = "REQUEST_CHANGES"
54		// VerdictBlock means escalation should stop; the bead is flagged for human review.
55		VerdictBlock Verdict = "BLOCK"
56	)
57	
58	// ReviewResult is the structured outcome of a post-merge bead review.
59	type ReviewResult struct {
60		Verdict          Verdict    `json:"verdict"`
61		Rationale        string     `json:"rationale,omitempty"`
62		PerAC            []ReviewAC `json:"per_ac,omitempty"`
63		RawOutput        string     `json:"raw_output,omitempty"`
64		ReviewerHarness  string     `json:"reviewer_harness,omitempty"`
65		ReviewerModel    string     `json:"reviewer_model,omitempty"`
66		ReviewerProvider string     `json:"reviewer_provider,omitempty"`
67		SessionID        string     `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
68		BaseRev          string     `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`
69		ResultRev        string     `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
70		ExecutionDir     string     `json:"execution_dir,omitempty"`
71		DurationMS       int        `json:"duration_ms,omitempty"`
72		Error            string     `json:"error,omitempty"`
73		// InputBytes and OutputBytes are the FEAT-022 §16 byte counters used to
74		// populate the compact summary appended to review and review-error event
75		// bodies. InputBytes is the assembled prompt size; OutputBytes is the
76		// raw reviewer output size (zero on pre-dispatch overflow / transport).
77		InputBytes  int `json:"input_bytes,omitempty"`
78		OutputBytes int `json:"output_bytes,omitempty"`
79	}
80	
81	type ReviewAC struct {
82		Number   int    `json:"number"`
83		Item     string `json:"item,omitempty"`
84		Grade    string `json:"grade,omitempty"`
85		Evidence string `json:"evidence,omitempty"`
86	}
87	
88	type reviewArtifactManifest struct {
89		Harness          string                     `json:"harness,omitempty"`
90		Model            string                     `json:"model,omitempty"`
91		SessionID        string                     `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
92		BaseRev          string                     `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`
93		ResultRev        string                     `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
94		Verdict          string                     `json:"verdict,omitempty"`
95		BeadID           string                     `json:"bead_id,omitempty"`
96		ExecutionDir     string                     `json:"execution_dir,omitempty"`
97		EvidenceAssembly *EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry `json:"evidence_assembly,omitempty"`
98	}
99	
100	type reviewArtifactResult struct {
101		Verdict          string                     `json:"verdict"`
102		PerAC            []ReviewAC                 `json:"per_ac,omitempty"`
103		Rationale        string                     `json:"rationale,omitempty"`
104		Findings         []Finding                  `json:"findings,omitempty"`
105		Error            string                     `json:"error,omitempty"`
106		EvidenceAssembly *EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry `json:"evidence_assembly,omitempty"`
107	}
108	
109	// SelectReviewerTier returns the tier to use for the review agent.
110	// Rule: max(impl_tier + 1, smart). Since smart is the ceiling, the
111	// reviewer always runs at smart tier regardless of the implementation tier.
112	func SelectReviewerTier(_ escalation.ModelTier) escalation.ModelTier {
113		return escalation.TierSmart
114	}
115

data.text:
Committed as `0a268e8`. All ACs met:

1. ✅ `cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go` exposes `try.Attempt(ctx, store, beadID, opts) (Outcome, error)`
2. ✅ Default path calls `agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig` + `agent.LandBeadResult` + optional `BeadReviewer` (Reviewer interface satisfied by `DefaultBeadReviewer`)
3. ✅ Returns Outcome via the C1 `ToOutcome` adapter
4. ✅ `TestAttempt_WrapsLegacyExecutor` covers stubbed-executor path (merged, park-needs-review, error propagation)
5. ✅ `go test ./internal/agent/try/` green; full repo build + lefthook checks green
6. ✅ No production callers — file is only referenced by its own test
